description: Capstone is a disassembly framework with the target of becoming the ultimate disassembly engine. Supports x86, ARM, ARM64, MIPS, PPC, SPARC, etc.

documentation.example: from capstone import Cs, CS_ARCH_X86, CS_MODE_64; md = Cs(CS_ARCH_X86, CS_MODE_64); [print(f'{i.address:#x}: {i.mnemonic} {i.op_str}') for i in md.disasm(b'\x90\x90', 0)]
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/capstone && python3 capstone.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/capstone && python3 capstone.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/capstone && python3 capstone.py --help`. Disassembly framework (multi-arch).
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the exploit category. Fits when the engagement needs: Disassembly framework (multi-arch).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Disassembly framework (multi-arch).

metadata.example: from capstone import Cs, CS_ARCH_X86, CS_MODE_64; md = Cs(CS_ARCH_X86, CS_MODE_64); [print(f'{i.address:#x}: {i.mnemonic} {i.op_str}') for i in md.disasm(b'\x90\x90', 0)]
